title: Component Audit Track C: Public AI-generated UI patterns collected from open web
summary: Collection of 25 original observations of recurring AI-generated UI component patterns across marketing pages, product apps, mobile interfaces, and enterprise dashboards. Observations gathered from public AI-generated UI galleries, open-source design system examples, and documented pattern libraries.
supportingClaims: AI-generated hero sections uniformly use centered gradient headlines with angled device mockups
Pricing tables default to three-column layout with highlighted 'Popular' tier
Feature grids always render as uniform card arrays
Dashboards always lead with four stat cards in a horizontal row
Sidebars default to persistent dark panels regardless of navigation depth
Navigation bars universally use logo-left, links-center, dual-buttons-right layout
Authentication screens use centered card on decorative gradient background
Settings pages default to sidebar-content split even for shallow hierarchies
Forms stack vertically with helper text on every field
Tables use zebra striping and pagination controls reflexively
Testimonials appear as carousel cards with circular headshots
Footers always use four-column layouts with social icons
Empty states default to illustration + 'Nothing here yet' messaging
Loading states use generic spinner or shimmer skeleton screens
Error pages prioritize large error codes and whimsical illustrations
Mobile navigation defaults to five-tab bottom bar
Onboarding always uses horizontal swipeable carousel format
Command palettes are added to every app regardless of action density
Analytics cards have identical sizing hiding metric priority
Status systems universally apply traffic-light color mapping
Search interfaces default to 'Ask anything...' placeholder with quick action chips
Modals always center with dark backdrop regardless of content type
Toast notifications use four-color system with auto-dismiss timers
Profile cards emphasize circular avatar with action button row
Progress bars default to thin brand-colored fill on gray track
